summary: Stop hook now teaches the live lane about completion review only when a ticket-backed impl run actually reaches the final completion gate, then requires the next final response to echo the nonce as a one-time completion password alongside the linked receipt.

rubric_sections.name: code-quality
findings: Password parsing and gating are localized to the completion-review path.
Regression coverage now distinguishes request, missing-password, and pass-through-to-orchestrator cases.

rubric_sections.name: integration-readiness
findings: The nonce/password path only activates after ticket resolution and impl-loop activation, so non-ticket stops still pass through untouched.
Receipt validation still remains the real completion gate; password text alone cannot complete the ticket.

rubric_sections.name: evidence-quality
findings: The ticket, memory, AGENTS, and review-gate docs all describe the same password-plus-receipt contract.
Verification covers syntax, targeted unit tests, metadata validation, and diff hygiene.

next_action: Close the ticket in the normal documenting flow when ready.
